key_points: No specific dorm praise or complaints surfaced in data
Campus described as gorgeous and well-maintained visually
Off-campus rentals in Lewisburg have some shady landlords
key_quotes: Don't rent from scott steeler or shing yeung. They are slum lords that will rip you off.
They've since been bought out & redone as of at least 2021 when I lived there.

key_points: Greek life dominates the social scene — if you're out, you're out
Freshman guys locked out of frat parties for an entire year
Sophomore rush means first year can feel socially isolated
key_quotes: That is so lame that freshman guys can't get into fraternity parties for a whole year — that could be a deal breaker!
Social scene revolves around the fraternities and sororities.
Rush is your sophomore year which can make freshman year a little tough especially if you are a guy.

key_points: Food described as awful, even by college standards
One current student planning to transfer cites food as a specific complaint
No positive dining comments found anywhere in the data
key_quotes: The food here is awful, even compared to other colleges.
I'm in the same situation as a freshman here, planning to transfer to a lower-ranked school where I connect more with the student life.

key_points: No dedicated mental health support data surfaced
Academically rigorous environment with grade pressure noted
Isolation of rural location could compound stress for some students
key_quotes: If you are afraid of hard work, don't go to Bucknell.
I'm in the same situation as a freshman here, planning to transfer to a lower-ranked school where I connect more with the student life.

key_points: Sticker price over $90,000/year — one of highest mentioned anywhere
Aid appeals sometimes work, but many report getting nothing extra
Merit scholarships exist up to $40k/year but are highly competitive
key_quotes: All in cost is over $90,000 per year now without aid.
Transferred from Bucknell because of the price, they gave me $500 off tuition.
My son was accepted last year and asked for more and got nothing.

key_points: Professor-student relationships are consistently the top praise
Engineering is rigorous — expect to fight for grades
Econ and CS programs are well-regarded with strong career outcomes
key_quotes: Professors will know you on a first name basis. I found they wanted to help you succeed.
I would say the engineering school definitely did not take it easy on students — you had to fight for Bs.
Bucknell has a great CS department. Smart, caring profs, constantly updating their courses, chances for research.

key_points: Board accused of gutting research funding and employee benefits
Tuition money reportedly spent on president's house and football stadium screen
Employee morale described as lowest in decades by insiders
key_quotes: Bucknell is in crisis mode because of the board and their administrative lackeys.
Tuition money is being used to upgrade the president's house and put a new megatron in the (losing) football team's stadium.
They've cut benefits, they've fired a ton of people, and they aren't supporting research.

key_points: Campus is genuinely beautiful — aerial footage and visitors agree
Lewisburg is tiny and rural; limited options off-campus
Being in the middle of nowhere can be fun or isolating depending on you
key_quotes: It's a gorgeous campus.
Being in the middle of nowhere is actually a lot of fun. Also club and intramural sports can be as competitive as you want.
I grew up in Lewisburg! It's a great place and the downtown area is so great and lots of fun!

key_points: Ranked #21 on LinkedIn Top Colleges for career success in 2025
Alumni network described as strong and actively helpful
Econ and business paths lead to solid finance and consulting outcomes
key_quotes: Bucknell alumni network is amazing.
My husband was an Econ major at Bucknell and is now a partner at a public finance firm.
Bucknell is absolutely the right place to find strong econ/business paths.

key_points: $90k/year sticker price is extremely hard to justify without significant aid
Strong career outcomes help ROI, but debt risk is real
Multiple students transferred or considered it due to cost alone
key_quotes: Personal opinion but 70k per year is too much for any school.
Transferred from Bucknell because of the price, they gave me $500 off tuition.
$70,000 is insane to pay for 4 years to only earn a bachelors degree.

key_points: Homogeneous, wealthy, preppy student body — very little diversity
Tight-knit community once you find your people, but that can take time
A Bucknell experience depends heavily on Greek life participation
key_quotes: The majority of the people here fit the 'rich kid' stereotype.
It has an unbelievably high rate of students marrying each other — probably has to do with very homogenous student body.
A progressive women's college in Southern California is very different than an apolitical LAC with lots of Greek life.

key_points: Administration: board cutting research, firing staff, spending on vanity projects
Culture: drunk students throwing beer and zyn on locals' cars most weekends
Diversity: student body described as overwhelmingly white, wealthy, and homogeneous
key_quotes: rapey white male supremacism
It's for sure the Bucknell students (and parents) that are the rudest out of anyone I handle day-to-day.
Bucknell is in crisis mode because of the board and their administrative lackeys.

key_points: Professor relationships are genuinely exceptional — lifelong connections reported
Sophomore rush means you build real friendships before Greek decisions
Undergrad research opportunities are real and accessible across departments
key_quotes: Some are life long colleagues and friends still!
The sophomore year rush — you will already have a group of friends from your first year.
Undergrad research is prevalent and professors often have personal investment in student success.
